reviews.title: Not durable, hard to latch
reviews.text:
I liked the shape of this band and how it looked on my sense. Unfortunately it was less than a week before it started snagging on blankets and towels. Then it started snagging my tighter woven clothes. Within a couple of weeks the area that was snagging on things bent outward resulting in it scratching myself and others along with snagging anything that got close to it.

It is also very hard to do the clasp with one hand. At least if you have a reduced level of manual dexterity. I did figure out how to put it on. Taking it off is easier.

I can't recommend this item. I sent it back for a refund.

reviews.title: Colorful band
reviews.text: I love the color . The band narrows once it comes off the fitbit. My only dislike is the extra band goes under the band instead of on top.

reviews.title: I have a love hate relationship with this band.
reviews.text: I love the look of this band on my Fitbit, it matches perfectly. How you fasten it is kind of a pain. It slips through and runs under itself, which prevents a floppy tail of band, but it can be tricky to do and adjust. Everything else about the band is great. It's lightweight, matches my Fitbit perfectly, and is easy to clean.

reviews.title: Nice Band
reviews.text: I like having options when it comes to the band of my Fitbit Versa3. This one looks nice. It's a little less casual than the silicone ones. It fits my Versa fine and was easy to attach. I wear my Fitbit on my left arm (I'm right handed). I found it a little more difficult to put on my wrist than my other bands. I was able to get it, but it took a few tries. Once on my wrist, my Versa feels secure and I'm not concerned about it coming undone.

reviews.title: Super Cute
reviews.text: Loving this wristband. It fits great and stays on without problems. Totally adjustable and wears really nicely. Couldn't be happier with this wristband.

reviews.title: Nice resolution
reviews.text:
I bought this watch because of the high-resolution display. And that's what I got, no more, no less.

Upon first impression, I can tell the display has crisp graphics. The touch screen is responsive. The processor is fast. Overall, the phone itself is nice.

The app, on the other hand, is awful. It is intuitive, but it loses Bluetooth connectivity without reason. This is the only app that I've seen that will move forward and backward during a dial sync. When I switch to certain dials, the progress bar will reach 61%, then count back down to 60%, then 59%, then start going back up. It's nice to see honesty in an app, but it's just strange.

I didn't test notifications from other apps, but no SMS text messages came through. The setup is simple and easy with SMS notifications settings on its own section. But once set up, no messages came through.

Battery life? What battery life? After a fully charge, this watch drained to 90% in the 15 minutes I used to overlook the features and set up the app. This from a 330mAh battery. At this rate, the battery will be depleted in less than two hours.

reviews.title: Junk
reviews.text: What a piece of junk. The instructions don't match the watch or the Amazon description and is written in awful broken English. And the app--- AWFUL. Navigation is bad, the English on the app is even worse than the instructions. I'm seriously concerned that downloading the app has somehow compromised anything on my phone. While I understand Amazon is a gigantic company that can't police every single item for sale, this is just BLATANTLY bad. In case my review isn't already clear, DO NOT BUY.

reviews.title: Great watch for the price
reviews.text: It was very difficult for me to set up this watch until I scanned the scan code from the pamphlet. The directions weren't easy. I think I would like a watch a little smaller but I like the shape and bands.  The price was great. I'm still not sure about the sleep mode, nothing seems to be registering. Steps are showing up which is cool, BPM is registering. Still figuring it out. Arrived promptly.  Additional info: the watch is NOT tracking my erratic sleep pattern, which I really needed it to do. I also found the size of the round watch intrusive to my long sleeve shirts and arm. Loved the pink and green bands and the color of the watch face. Steps are also not being accurately recorded. So sad, really had high hopes for this.

reviews.title: Worth the money n saved my life
reviews.text: Love the feminine color. Easy to see. Helps me keep track of certain health irregularities as they happen therefore possibly averting a health emergency. Ty for having an ingenious machine in such a lovely and compact device.

reviews.title: I was worried that this cable damaged my watch.
reviews.text: I bought this item as extra charging cables for my Vivomove Style, which has physical watch hands in addition to the electronic display. Every time I use this charger instead of the OEM charger, the time shown by the physical hands is hours off and I have to realign.  It also seems to become much less sensitive to touch until I plug it back into the OEM charger to reset it.  The Vivomove Style will keep the time on the physical hands 2 weeks after the battery dies (and I've never seen it not keep accurate time), so it wasn't a dead battery issue.  I was hoping to have more than one cable to charge my watch but based on the issues I'm having,  I'm going back to the OEM cable.

reviews.title: Watch is good, not great with mybphone
reviews.text:
The watch is great. Synched quickly with the app and I like the number of different face options offered. The battery life is also strong and it charges quickly.
Things I didn't like. If you touch the face it quickly unlocks and scrolls through the menus. So when I cross my arms I can mess up the watch, it needs a better lock. The app didn't synch all options to my watch/phone. So, I couldn't track sleep, heartrate and other things.
Maybe if I have a better phone?

reviews.title: Kids love it
reviews.text: It is a generic version of a smart watch but the kids don’t realize it as it works almost exactly as the real thing. It tells the time, lets you make and receive calls and text and you download the app just like you would with the real thing. It’s easy to set up. Comes with a charging cord. Connects to any phone. #FoundItOnAmazon

reviews.title: Great Apple alternative!
reviews.text: Got this for my mom, here it is next to my Apple Watch, it has pretty much all the same features as the Apple at about a quarter of the price. The battery lasts all day and seems to be on par with the battery life of my watch. The app is easy to use and my mom loves it!

reviews.title: Awesome Smart Watch
reviews.text:
For the money, this is an awesome smart watch that has some of the same popular features as that “fruit” brand.
The watch was packaged securely in a box and included a User Manual and a “How to Connect with the smart watch” brochure.

My watch was received with 46% battery life and did not need to be charged immediately, but I noticed that the charge cord is magnetic and attaches easily to the back of the watch.

I read the instructions and powered on the watch. I downloaded the app and proceeded to pair, referred to as “bind” in the manual. The smart watch paired effortlessly with my iPhone 13. Other than pairing or unpairing, the user manual doesn’t cover much else, but I found the watch use to be intuitive. I do think a more detailed user manual would be helpful to explain navigating and swipe direction of the screens.

The watch has many functions and I was pleasantly surprised that the watch provided a “Siri connection” and performed flawlessly. I compared the blood pressure and oxygen sensors to my individual monitors to check accuracy. Surprisingly, the watch is pretty accurate.

As I mentioned earlier, I started using the watch with 46% battery life and wore the watch for approximately 25 hours before I received a low battery alert.

This is a great buy and so much so, that within hours of using my watch, I ordered another one for my husband.

reviews.title: Best watch yet!
reviews.text: I'm very impressed with this smart watch. It has so many features and works well. Definitely recommend it.

reviews.title: Glitchy smartwatch has a mind of its own
reviews.text:
I've been using this smartwatch for about five weeks. There are tons of watch faces to choose from, but I couldn't find any with just the info I wanted at a glance: time, date, steps, and weather. I found one watch face with everything but weather and loaded that, but the watch face spontaneously changes several times a day to designs I've never selected. I don't like having to reset it with my phone every few hours to get back to my preferred watch face.

It vibrates at random times and shows me (what I believe is) some sort of prompt to reset it. Sometimes it prompts me for voice commands or makes phone calls for no apparent reason. I erased my contacts, so I hope it won't accidentally wrist-dial anyone else.

It needs to be recharged every 2-3 days — not every ten days as claimed for "Bluetooth daily usage mode" — and I'm NOT using the majority of its supposed features.

I've reached out to customer support a few times, but they say I'm doing something wrong. I'm just wearing it; the glitches happen whether I'm moving or still. Support has been no help at all. I haven't been able to find information on how to make it work as advertised online either, but at least Google doesn't blame me for the faulty product.

reviews.title: Un-binds Constantly...
reviews.text: It wont update, most of the watch faces don't download correctly. It decides on it's own when to un-bind from blu tooth. The only reason i'm not returning is because it's comfortable and it tracks my steps and tells time. Literally the only things I use. However, you do not receive messages, you miss calls, and it struggles to bind to your phone. I have had to re-install the app numerous times. When i complained to the online chat. they suggested re-installing the app. That was their only answer to any questions. Doesn't seem like anyone knows really what's going on. either way, if you want to "phone/messages" part of the watch, i highly suggest shopping for a different one.

reviews.title: Face size is great for reading it without glasses
reviews.text: I like being able to see the face and read it without my glasses. I didn't turn on any of the special features like phone, pictures, messaging, etc; mainly because it's made in China, but I'm sure I'm giving away info anyway. Unlike the Fitbit, this doesn't have a way to list and track your food intake and glucose readings. I bought it inorder to read the time, steps, etc, without glasses.

title: Samsung Galaxy Watch3 2020 Smartwatch (Bluetooth + Wi-Fi + GPS) International Model (Bronze, 41mm)
description: The only smartwatch with a precise rotating bezel for navigating between apps. Larger screen, but lighter and thinner for more comfort: With its subtle rotating bezel, it's easier to see and focus on what's important on an even larger screen. The Galaxy Watch3 is thinner and smaller, and up to 15% lighter with a sophisticated look that, thanks to its reduced frame, is perfect for the wrist. Breathe easier with built-in oxygen measurement: The Galaxy Watch3 uses red LEDs and infrared rays to calculate your SpO2 levels or the amount of oxygen in your blood. Because oxygen saturation is directly related to physical activity, you can use the Galaxy Watch3 to push your routine to the next level. Enjoy a better sleep: The Galaxy Watch3 records your active sleep, deep sleep, and total sleep time to keep track and improve the quality of your sleep. Samsung partnered with the National Sleep Foundation to include educational content and provide tips on your device to help you sleep better. Do your best and then repeat it: With over 120 home workout programs to choose from, the Galaxy Watch3 helps you move your body at your own pace. Pick one from your phone and stream it on your TV. It will show you your heart rate in real-time while you exercise. Use the Ridge Sport strap for even more accurate stats. Continue your conversations: When you receive a new message, the Galaxy Watch3 shows your conversation history so you can follow them. You can also view images without needing an extra touch, making it easier to keep up while you're working out. Add some context with the smart answer: Respond to messages at all times and without missing a beat. The Galaxy Watch3's smart reply analyzes the texts and photos you receive and gives you possible answers in different categories, such as food, celebrations, selfies and much more.
features: 1.2 "(30.2mm) Super AMOLED, 360 x 360px Resolution, 16M Colors, CPU: Dual-Core 1.15GHz, 340mAh Battery, Tizen OS, Bluetooth, Wi-Fi
The Galaxy Watch3 uses red LEDs and infrared rays to calculate your SpO2 levels or the amount of oxygen in your blood.

5 ATM/IP68 The water and dust resistance rating, Military-grade durability, Larger screen but lighter and thinner for more comfort.
International Model No Warranty in the US. Compatible with Android and iOS devices. Samsung Pay - Not Supported.

title: Apple Watch Series 8 [GPS + Cellular 41mm] Smart Watch w/Graphite Stainless Steel Case w/Midnight Sport Band-S/M. Fitness Tracker, Blood Oxygen & ECG Apps, Always-On Retina Display, Water Resistant
features: WHY APPLE WATCH SERIES 8 — Your essential companion for a healthy life is now even more powerful. Advanced sensors provide insights to help you better understand your health. New safety features can get you help when you need it. The bright, Always-On Retina display is easy to read, even when your wrist is down.

CELLULAR CONNECTIVITY — Send a text, make a call, and stream music without your iPhone, even while traveling internationally. Use Family Setup to manage Apple Watch for family members who don’t have an iPhone.
EASILY CUSTOMIZABLE — Available in a range of sizes and materials, with dozens of bands to choose from and watch faces with complications tailored to whatever you’re into.

INNOVATIVE SAFETY FEATURES — Crash Detection and Fall Detection can automatically connect you with emergency services in the event of a severe car crash or a hard fall. And Emergency SOS provides urgent assistance with the press of a button.

ADVANCED HEALTH FEATURES — Temperature sensing is a breakthrough feature that provides deep insights into women’s health. Keep an eye on your blood oxygen. Take an ECG anytime. Get notifications if you have an irregular rhythm. And see how much time you spent in REM, Core, or Deep sleep with Sleep Stages.

SIMPLY COMPATIBLE — It works seamlessly with your Apple devices and services. Unlock your Mac automatically. Find your devices with a tap. Pay and send money with Apple Pay. Apple Watch requires an iPhone 8 or later with the latest iOS version.
INCREDIBLE DURABILITY — Tougher than tough. It’s crack resistant, IP6X-certified dust resistant, and swimproof with WR50 water resistance.

A POWERFUL FITNESS PARTNER — The enhanced Workout app gives you new ways to train, and advanced metrics for more information about your workout performance. And Apple Watch comes with three months of Apple Fitness+ free.
APPS ON YOUR WRIST — With access to tens of thousands of apps on the App Store right on your wrist, Apple Watch is the perfect personal device for whatever you love to do.
LEGAL DISCLAIMERS — This is a summary of the main product features. See below to learn more.

reviews.title: Love this watch
reviews.text: Nice looking watch with accurate steps. I love the gps feature where it shows your walk on a true map on the app. Nice visual. Heart rate is very accurate as well. Haven't used music feature as much as I thought I would. Garmin makes a nice watch. Highly recommend.

reviews.title: Heart rate way off
reviews.text:
The main thing I bought this for is to monitor heart rate while hiking. Its reading is way off for me - as confirmed by a pulse ox and manually counting. The watch kept telling me my heart rate was over 100 just around the house. I have nsvt but my heart rate generally runs low. So I'm very sensitive when I have palpitations - when this watch kept saying my heart rate was over 95 I wasnt buying it. That brings into question any other readings or parameters.

I find the watch annoying and frustrating to use. The watch face does not interact very well for me. I'm constantly trying to tap it and it wont register so then the screen shuts off. Another annoying thing is it tells you to start moving around when I'm doing housework. I guess you can shut that off but I've really lost interest. Hopefully Garmin can offer some fix for the reading since its past the return date.

The positives - watch is comfortable and I don't wear jewelry. I forget its on. Its a large size so its readable if you have vision issues. I've had no problem with that.

reviews.title: Lightweight and perfect for small wrists

reviews.title: Works well for step counting and can measure heartrate upon request. Otherwise less than stellar
reviews.text:
This generally works well enough for exercise purposes. I didn't get the "dangerous app warning" on my iPhone, though I did get a warning that if I use the watch to display messages, then the app has access to all my contacts and the text of all my messages. I doubt I'm a target for Chinese spying so I chose to ignore that, but it's still worth noting.

I love that it's waterproof, other watch faces are available (though I couldn't figure out how to restore the original, more-helpful one without doing a full factory reset), it tracks steps and sleep, plus it's able to measure heartrate and blood pressure. Mine sometimes tells me the weather, but only immediately after connecting to my Google account on my phone; otherwise it just says "unknown" (and honestly, if I have to click extra buttons on my phone, I can just check the weather there). It has a music playback control, but I couldn't get that to work. It also theoretically connects to my phone and lets me enter a phone number, but I couldn't click "dial" so that bit felt more like a child's toy, where you pretend to call a phone but don't actually do it. But since I use this for exercise more than connectivity, I'm OK with that.

Some things are less than stellar, like their heart rate screen always shows a default chart (I don't think it ever lets you create your own heart rate chart)... and it shows a heartrate from 120-150 ALWAYS, which catches me off guard when I've been sitting at my desk for 2 hours. But I learned it's just a concept; there is no real *graph of my heartrate that I can find, just current / min / max that I can see from my phone. Or I can click "measure" to get my instant heartrate on my phone (good while exercising). Blood pressure can sometimes be measured from your phone (though it takes 1-4 minutes for some reason), and sometimes that screen disappears completely; I haven't yet figured out the pattern

It doesn't seem to measure many things other than steps automatically; you have to select each one when you're wanting to see it. Soooooooo all told it's solidly mediocre. It's good for counting steps, manually checking heartrate, and manually checking blood pressure (very slowly) on occasion; otherwise it's a lot weaker than most smart phones

reviews.title: Poor Measurements - Support Cannot Help
reviews.text:
After charging for 30 minutes as instructed, I paired the smart watch with the app. This was easy, and the one reason I gave it 2 stars instead of 1. The other reason for 2 stars, the interface was easier to use than I expected.

But if the measurements are not accurate, ease of use doesn't matter.

I bought this smart watch to track my heart rate while exercising—mainly walking. After recent heart procedures, my doctor wants me to keep my pulse below a certain rate. Each time I measure, the pulse differs substantially from the pulse oximeter that always agrees with my doctor's office pulse oximeter, and heart monitor. It goes as high as 90 when the PO says 52. I took multiple measurements, including taking my own pulse. The closest measurements were 60 when the actual rate was 51 or 52.

Support is through a chat on the app. I did not wait long for support, however they did not try to help me. They insisted that the heart rate changes. That I had to let it sync with the app. Not once did they address the possibility that the smart watch might be reading the rate wrong. Told me to try again after I had already tried multiple times.

Focusing on anything but the issue I am reporting does not help me. Even suggesting I exercise and measure my heart rate. Telling me the human heart rate is constantly changing. Literally, answers like they were talking to a small child.

I had high hopes for this device. It disappointed on two levels - Heart Rate, Support. I cannot recommend. Look for a different smart watch if you need accurate heart rate measurements.

reviews.title: NOT AS DESCRIBED AT ALL
reviews.text: I carefully read all reviews before purchasing this smart watch but was so disappointed. It would not hold preferences & kept defaulting to kg & cm. Would not toggle from one thing to another on app. Had to go all the way out of app & go back in before it would toggle from steps to profile to anything else. Sometimes I'd get my cell to ring on my watch, never could answer it or text back from the watch. I'd rather pay the extra & get an apple watch that actually works. I can't tell you how many times I set the preferences & saved them only to find it defaulted right back the next day.  One of the sorriest watches I've ever owned & I kept it 2 weeks before returning it really trying to make it work.

reviews.title: Better than name brand. Long battery life!!
reviews.text: I will take this watch over a fit bit any day! In fact my husband bought me a fit bit and I returned it because thus watch is soooo much better. What I like most is the BATTERY LIFE IS AMAZING! I bought it weeks ago and have only charged it twice.

reviews.title: Never dies
reviews.text: This thing is great.  Does everything you would expect and the battery lasts FOREVER!!  Great smartwatch.

reviews.title: Sleep function is worthless
reviews.text: I really like this watch, has everything I wanted and everything works with one exception:  the sleep analysis feature is worthless.  It only records sleep between 10:00 pm and 8:00 am and if you move or get up it screws up everything. Good customer service, they really try to help, but they will tell you if you get up to go to the bathroom, watch begins recording all over again. Too bad!

reviews.title: Great looking watch, battery life isn't what I expected
reviews.text: The major feature that seems missing from this watch is sleep tracking.  Reviews of the watch point to the limited battery life of the watch, and not being enough to track though an average night's sleep.

reviews.title: Measure your wrist before you buy.
reviews.text:
The blue silicone band was too small to go around my wrist. I called Skagen and they offered to sell me a bigger band. I told them I shouldn't have to pay extra for the watch to go on my wrist. They said since I bought it from Amazon there was nothing they could do. So sadly, this is getting returned.
I've worn plenty of watches before, and I don't have a fatty wrist. My wrist is 8 inches in circumference, which is average for a male.
The interesting thing about the size as I inspected and measured the watch, is that if you measure the watch laid out flat, 8 inches goes to the second to last notch, and it should fit fine. BUT -- the watch body itself obviously does not bend, which means in actual wear when the watch goes AROUND your wrist, you need an additional 3/4 to 1 inch for it to work.
It's basically like that one gallon jug I bought at Walmart, I got it home to make a drink mix in it just to realize that yes, it held one gallon, if you filled it to the absolute brim, at which point putting in the drink mix made it spill.
If your wrist is greater than 7 inches in circumference, I would not buy this watch.

reviews.title: It's ok. But you can get a Fitbit style watch for less
reviews.text: I wear it near my pulse because I feel it gives you a more accurate reading. The sleep monitor sucks. It didn't start monitoring my sleep until I turned it around and put the sensors on my inner wrist

reviews.title: Sent back twice because the blood pressure measurement was inaccurate
reviews.text: I had some confidence in buying this at first.  It connected to app nicely and did well with sleep measurement when I tried that.  I also liked the face choices.  However, having a reasonably accurate blood pressure measurement as long as it is included as a feature was very important for me.  It always read about the same measurement of 140/86, but that was not accurate when compared to a cuff style.  It also read the same for my wife who is always different.  I returned one, got an easy replacement and had the same issue.  Returned for refund this time.  I did not check if waterproof, but that was also an important feature.
